title: paeonia officinalis subsp. broteri
intro: Paeonia officinalis subsp. broteri, also known as the Portuguese peony or Garden peony, is a subspecies of the perennial herb Paeonia officinalis, native to Western Europe and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which are attributed to its active compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Paeonia officinalis subsp. broteri include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, as well as its antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In herbal medicine, the root of the plant is typically used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
